rationale_for_baseline_tier: Uncomplicated vulvovaginal candidiasis with typical symptoms and no PID/STI features is benign and appropriately managed with an antifungal via telehealth, with routine follow-up if not resolving (T1). PID/STI/alternative diagnosis routes to urgent review (T3/T4); pregnancy/recurrence route to GP for tailored management.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever, deep pelvic or lower-abdominal pain, abnormal/intermenstrual/postcoital bleeding, or an offensive or frothy discharge
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests PID or an STI/alternative infection needing assessment, testing and different treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a fever, deep pelvic or lower-tummy pain, abnormal bleeding, or an offensive/frothy discharge — see a doctor urgently, as that suggests a different or deeper infection.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: You could be pregnant, or this recurs four or more times a year, or doesn't respond
escalation_edges.rationale: Pregnancy changes the antifungal choice; recurrence warrants investigating an underlying cause (e.g. diabetes).

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; very distressed by the itch
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Reassure and give clear PID/STI and pregnancy-related advice.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is thrush after your antibiotics. An antifungal (a tablet or a cream/pessary) clears it and the itch/burning should ease within a day or two. Wear cotton underwear, avoid scented products and douching, and wash with water only.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Antifungal + telehealth/GP follow-up; urgent GP for PID/STI features; GP if pregnant/recurrent
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Urgent care/GP for fever, deep pelvic pain, abnormal bleeding or offensive/frothy discharge.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Vulvovaginal candidiasis (post-antibiotic); BV/trichomonas/STI and PID screened negative; antifungal (confirm pregnancy for drug choice); STI/PID and recurrence safety-nets given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating as thrush with no PID/STI safety-net or pregnancy/drug check.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A PID/STI is mislabelled as thrush and goes untreated (infertility risk), or oral fluconazole is given in an unrecognised pregnancy.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not distinguishing from BV/trichomonas / not checking pregnancy.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED for uncomplicated thrush — unnecessary (telehealth antifungal is appropriate).
